# SeeLinks Web UI Reference

This note captures the original SeeLinks web interface as a visual specification for the HMRC narrative workbench UI pass.

Source snapshot: original SeeLinks branch `datapacks`, commit `7983b2e` (`Add geography ontology graph + rollup UI`). The source files read for this note were `docs/ui-current.md`, `docs/ui-current-callouts.md`, `docs/user-guide.md`, and `apps/web/src/routes/+page.svelte`. Local uncommitted SeeLinks changes in devcontainer files were not used.

## Infographics

- [Web UI anatomy](../assets/infographics/seelinks-web-ui-anatomy.svg)
- [Facet and tile interaction model](../assets/infographics/seelinks-facet-tile-interactions.svg)
- [Views, detail surfaces, and output tools](../assets/infographics/seelinks-views-detail-output.svg)
- [Data, state, and export flow](../assets/infographics/seelinks-data-state-flow.svg)

## Complete UI Inventory

### Global Layout

- Resizable left sidebar.
- Main workspace.
- View title and mode description.
- View-mode tabs: Grid, Outline, Graph, Timeline, Reading.
- Dataset picker.
- Load JSON/JSONL.
- Load API example.
- About/intro launcher when pack intro content exists.
- Sticky search and action bar.
- Optional intro page.
- Selection summary.
- Optional collection profile.
- Optional print/import/service-worker status messages.
- History bar.
- Grid, detail dock, and alternate view surfaces.

### Sidebar Panels

- Order by panel with multi-facet sort stack.
- Sort precedence chip and direction toggle.
- Per-sort legend for including or excluding values.
- Facets panel with metadata toggle.
- Facet open/close.
- Facet pin/unpin.
- Facet drag-reorder.
- Facet sort/order-by add/remove.
- Facet value auto-order and clear-order.
- Facet value drag-reorder.
- Links panel summarising related facet values.
- MCP Packs panel when `VITE_MCP_BASE_URL` is configured.
- Collections panel with collection name input, Save from selection, and Open.
- Printing panel with print limit and Print selection.
- AI export panel with Copy AI context and Download AI context.
- Tile Text panel for title field, subtitle field, title template, small ID badge, and reset.
- Editing panel for mode, property, value, and Apply to selected.

### Facet Interactions

- Click a facet header to open or close it.
- Pin a facet to keep it open.
- Drag a facet header to reorder facets.
- Drag a facet name onto the tile grid to pin that facet, open it, and colour tiles by that facet's value order.
- Click a facet value to set the primary highlight.
- Right-click a facet value to Adjust, adding or removing highlight values within that facet.
- Double-click a facet value to choose that highlighted value and reduce the current view.
- Drag facet values to reorder values.
- Drag a facet value onto the tile grid to create a rollup card and apply the related colour legend.
- Highlight counts switch from total counts to highlighted/total counts when highlighting is active.

### Tile And Grid Interactions

- Single-click a tile to toggle manual selection.
- Double-click a tile to open the docked detail card.
- Selected, highlighted, active/open, missing-image, and coloured-by-facet states are visually distinct.
- Tiles can show image/icon, small ID badge, title, subtitle, category path, order-by summary, tile colour reason, and state text.
- The grid accepts facet drops and facet-value drops.
- Rollup cards show value, count, fraction, progress bar, Expand/Collapse, Remove, and Clear all.

### View Reduction And History

- Choose highlighted reduces the current view to highlighted records.
- Choose unhighlighted reduces the current view to non-highlighted records.
- If no facet highlight is active, those commands operate on manual tile selection.
- Each reduction pushes a history entry containing label, count, total, polarity, facet/value metadata, and highlight snapshot.
- Undo view steps back through history.
- History chips reopen earlier reductions.
- Reset clears filters and returns to the full pack.

### Detail Surface

- Docked detail panel appears above the grid when an item is open.
- Tile strip supports quick stepping through the current set.
- Previous, Next, and Close controls sit in the detail header.
- Tabs: Overview and Images.
- Double-clicking a hero image opens the Images tab.
- Overview content is pack-specific: UK Public AI, World Pulse, Wikipedia, Micropedia, Pond Watch, API calls, or generic summary.
- Images tab renders gallery images with captions where available.

### Data And Output

- Static local packs can be loaded from `/data/...`.
- URL `?pack=...` can choose a pack path.
- Drag/drop or file input can load JSON, JSONL, full `pack.json`, plain item arrays, or OpenAPI 3.x specs.
- MCP resources can be loaded when an MCP HTTP endpoint is configured.
- Intro Markdown can be shown on first load and reopened.
- Collections persist locally.
- Tile/facet/sort preferences persist locally.
- Print selection uses print templates.
- AI export includes current records plus selected/highlighted flags.

## HMRC Workbench Implication

The HMRC workbench should move closer to this model by keeping a dense left control rail, making the card grid the primary interaction surface, preserving visible state transitions, supporting facet-colour and rollup gestures, and treating source-card links, context export, and narrative thumbnails as outputs of the same card-selection workflow.
